origin.question: [µ¢ćõ╗Č 1: 1_s16_16.2.md]\n\nProblem 16.2. Suppose that an observer, at rest at some point inside a spherical relativistic star, measures the radial pressure-buoyant force, $\mathbf{F}_{\mathrm{buoy}}$ , on a small element of volume V, using the usual laboratory techniques. What value will he find for $\mathbf{F}_{\mathrm{buoy}}$ , in terms of $\rho$ , p, m, V, and dp/dr? If he equates this buoyant force to an equal and opposite gravitational force, $\mathbf{F}_{\mathrm{grav}}$ , what will $\mathbf{F}_{\mathrm{grav}}$ be in terms of $\rho$ , p, m, V, and r? How do these results differ from the corresponding Newtonian results?
origin.solution:
## Solution

Solution 16.2. The observer makes measurements in his local orthonormal
frame $\mathbf{e}_{\hat{\mathfrak{t}}} = \mathbf{e}^{-\Phi}\mathbf{e}_{\mathfrak{t}},\quad \mathbf{e}_{\hat{\mathfrak{r}}} = \mathbf{e}^{-\lambda}\mathbf{e}_{\mathfrak{r}},\quad \mathbf{e}_{\hat{\boldsymbol{\theta}}} = \mathbf{r}^{-1}\mathbf{e}_{\boldsymbol{\theta}},\quad \mathbf{e}_{\hat{\boldsymbol{\phi}}} = (\mathbf{r}\sin \theta)^{-1}\mathbf{e}_{\boldsymbol{\phi}};$
$$
\tilde {\omega}^{\hat {t}} = e^{\Phi} \widetilde {d t}, \quad \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {r}} = e^{\lambda} \widetilde {d r}, \quad \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\theta}} = r \widetilde {d \theta}, \quad \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\phi}} = r \sin \theta d \widetilde {\phi}.
$$
Here the metric is as given in the introduction, except $(1 - 2\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{r})^{-1}\equiv \mathrm{e}^{2\lambda}$ . The volume of a fluid element which he measures is
$$
\begin{array}{l}
V = \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {r}} \wedge \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\theta}} \wedge \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {\phi}} \\
= e^{\lambda} r^{2} \sin \theta \widetilde {d r} \wedge \widetilde {d \theta} \wedge \widetilde {d \phi} \\
= e^{\lambda} r^{2} \sin \theta d r d \theta d \phi . \\
\end{array}
$$
The pressure at opposite points on the vertical walls of the fluid element are the same, but because of the radial pressure gradient, the top and bottom pressures are not:
$$
\left| p_{t o p} - p_{b o t t o m} \right| = \left| p_{, r} \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {r}} \right| = \left| p_{, r} e^{- \lambda} \tilde {\omega}^{\hat {r}} \right|.
$$
Thus,
$$
\begin{array}{l}
| \mathbf {F}_{\text {b u o y ,}} |= | (\mathrm {p}_{\text {t o p}} - \mathrm {p}_{\text {b o t t o m}}) \times \text {a r e a} | \\
= | \mathbf {p}_{r, r} e^{- \lambda_{\omega} \hat {r}_{\omega} \hat {\theta}_{\omega} \hat {\phi}} | \\
= | p_{r} e^{- \lambda} V |. \\
\end{array}
$$
Since the direction of the buoyant force is radial,
$$
\mathbf {F}_{\text {b u o y ,}} = \mathrm {e}^{- \lambda} \mathrm {p}_{, r} \mathrm {V e}_{\hat {\mathrm {r}}} = \left(1 - \frac {2 \mathrm {m}}{\mathrm {r}}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}} \mathrm {p}_{, r} \mathrm {V e}_{\hat {\mathrm {r}}} .
$$
Using the OV equation for $\mathbf{p}_{\cdot ,\mathbf{r}}$ we find
$$
\mathbf {F}_{\text {g r a v .}} = - \mathbf {F}_{\text {b u o y .}} = - \frac {(\rho + p) (m + 4 \pi r^{3} p) V e_{\mathrm {f}}}{r^{2} \left(1 - \frac {2 m}{r}\right)^{\frac {1}{2}}}
$$
The Newtonian result is
$$
\mathrm {p}_{\mathrm {r}} \mathrm {V e}_{\mathrm {r}} = - \frac {\rho \mathrm {m V}}{\mathrm {r}^{2}} \mathrm {e}_{\mathrm {r}}^{\prime}.
$$
